input.initialPrompt: Run coverage analysis on this project. Coverage data is already in TestResults/coverage.cobertura.xml.

arguments.command: which dotnet reportgenerator 2>/dev/null; dotnet tool list -g 2>/dev/null | grep -i report; pip show pycobertura 2>/dev/null

data.reasoningText: The user wants me to run coverage analysis on the project. They mention that coverage data is already in `TestResults/coverage.cobertura.xml`. Let me look at what's available and generate a coverage report from that data.

arguments.command: which dotnet reportgenerator 2>/dev/null; dotnet tool list -g 2>/dev/null | grep -i report; pip show pycobertura 2>/dev/null

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-ff4452b75523417aaeee226349795cab && cat TestResults/coverage.cobertura.xml | head -50", "description": "Inspect coverage XML file", "initial_wait": 10}

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "which dotnet reportgenerator 2>/dev/null; dotnet tool list -g 2>/dev/null | grep -i report; pip show pycobertura 2>/dev/null", "description": "Check available coverage tools", "initial_wait": 10}

data.content:
Here's the coverage summary:

**Coverage Report — ContosoUniversity**

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| **Line coverage** | **42.5%** |
| Covered lines | 20 / 47 |
| Method coverage | 100% (4/4) |
| Classes | 1 (`StudentService`) |

**Breakdown by method:**
- `Enroll` — 75% line rate, 67% branch rate
- `CalculateGpa` — 25% line rate, 20% branch rate ← biggest gap

The `CalculateGpa` method has the most uncovered code. Improving tests there would have the highest impact on overall coverage.
